As per the handover, users were unable to make outbound calls, and the toll-free number 877-784-2288 was appearing as spam.

Configured all user extensions affected by the outbound issue and performed test calls — confirmed that outbound calls were working.

Asked Mike to verify, but he reported that the issue persisted. Upon further investigation, found that Mike’s desk phone IP was blocked due to repeated failed registration attempts with an incorrect username and password.

Accessed Mike’s system, performed a factory reset on the desk phone, and re-registered it successfully. Mike confirmed that users were now able to make outbound calls and that his desk phone was working properly.

However, after some time, Mike reported that users Melissa Goodyear and Sandra Mendoza were still unable to make outbound calls.

Waiting for the Didforsale vendor update.....

Connected to their systems, re-provisioned their 3CX apps, and tested outbound calls — but the issue persisted. Also reconfigured their extensions for testing, with the same result.

Contacted DIDforSale support and spoke with Sam, explained the issue, and he confirmed their team would investigate further from their end.

Regarding the “Scam Likely” message on T-Mobile numbers, Sam advised reaching out to T-Mobile directly to remove the spam tag.

Contacted T-Mobile support, but after reviewing the issue, they confirmed that this needs to be handled by the service provider (DIDforSale).

Title: VAF - 2 year email archive
Notes:
As per Sunny’s request, logged into the Microsoft 365 Exchange Admin Center to enable the 2-year email archive policy for VAF users. Accessed the Admin console → Exchange Admin Center → Recipients → Mailboxes, and selected the user account.

Under the Mailbox tab, enabled the Mailbox Archive option by switching the Mailbox archive status to “Enabled.” Verified that the archive mailbox was successfully created and labeled as “In-Place Archive.”

Additionally, confirmed that the VAF 2-year retention policy has been applied under the mailbox policies section. This ensures that all emails older than two years will automatically move to the archive mailbox as per organizational retention requirements.

After completing these configurations, verified the settings for accuracy and ensured that the archive mailbox is functioning properly. Finally, updated Sunny regarding the completion and successful verification of the requested configuration.Updated toSunny and Mike on mail.

Title: Team   we are not able to make outbound calls to the following numbers can you look into why?
Notes:
Mike reported an issue with outgoing calls on two numbers. We configured the VAF extension on the local environment, tested with a call, and captured the event logs. The log showed:

Event ID 30052 – “There was no user or outbound rule found for the number 310970469 that Ext. 509 dialed.”

We checked further and found that the issue occurs only when dialing with +1; without the “1” the call works. We reviewed the outbound rule, which is already set as the default for 11-digit numbers.

We have opened a case with DID to investigate why the call is failing when “1” is added.

Case ID: 72006

Title: HP Color LaserJet Pro M478f - Printer Setup
Notes:
Ticket Summary – Printer Setup Request (No Wi-Fi Capability)

Issue Reported

As per Mike, printer setup assistance was required for Michel. The printer had lost configuration or could not be detected.

Actions Taken

  • Connected to Michel's system remotely to identify and set up the printer.

  • Attempted to detect the printer, but it was not discoverable on the network.

  • Initiated a video call with Michel via Microsoft Teams to further investigate the issue.

  • Checked Windows printer settings and verified that the printer did not have Wi-Fi capability.

Technical Verification

  • Reviewed the printer’s technical specifications on the manufacturer’s official website.

  • Confirmed that the printer only supports:

    • Hi-Speed USB 2.0

    • Built-in Gigabit Ethernet (10/100/1000 Base-Tx)

    • Easy-access USB

  • Verified that Wi-Fi is not supported.

Next Steps

  • Communicated the findings to Mike.

  • Mike will arrange a physical Ethernet cable connection to the printer and follow up the next day for setup continuation.

Title: 3CX Issue 800-841-3000   when we dial this number from the following extension 302 - 307 the call can not be made   but if we dial the same number from  506, 204, 207 the call goes through   please check and let me know
Notes: During the investigation of customer reports that outbound calls to certain toll-free numbers (GEICO 800-841-3000 and AAA 877-422-2100) were either playing the message “Your call did not go through…” before eventually connecting, or were connecting briefly and then disconnecting, extensive troubleshooting was carried out. Outbound rules in 3CX were reviewed and confirmed to be properly configured for all extensions. A comparison of multiple extensions showed that the issue occurred more often when using the main toll-free number 877-784-2288 as the outbound caller ID, while extensions using standard DID numbers such as 949-989-7872 were not affected. Testing confirmed that the SIP carrier is taking longer to return SIP 180/183 call-progress responses for certain toll-free destinations when the main number is presented, causing 3CX to play the failure audio prematurely even though the call eventually completes. In the case of AAA (877-422-2100), the far-end carrier sends an early disconnect after call pickup, which results in the immediate call drop experienced by the user. The SIP trunk template currently in use does not expose advanced early-media or progress-tone handling options, so any improvements in signaling behavior must come from a 3CX software update or carrier-side adjustments. As an immediate workaround, affected extensions were temporarily configured to use a validated DID to ensure consistent signaling and prevent premature disconnects. The after-hours team has been requested to update 3CX to the latest version to ensure optimal SIP handling and compatibility with current carrier signaling standards. Next steps include retesting after the update is completed, monitoring call behavior to GEICO and AAA, and confirming stability across all affected extensions. The issue remains open pending completion of the system update and further verification.

Title: Microsoft 365 Uninstall and Reinstallation (Full Clean Install) on -VAF-HQ-LT-20
Notes:
Mike reported issue with Outlook classic after connected found Microsoft 365 due to a corrupted and partially-installed Office environment. Initial uninstall attempts failed, including standard removal from Programs & Features and multiple Click-to-Run uninstall commands. Winget and OfficeC2RClient were unable to detect or remove the installation, indicating a broken or incomplete Office deployment.

Performed the following billable tasks:

  1. Troubleshooting Office uninstall failure

    • Verified installation type (C2R vs MSI).

    • Tested PowerShell uninstall commands and OfficeC2RClient methods—no response from the system.

    • Attempted winget uninstall—Office package not detected.

    • Confirmed removal tool and SaRA limitations on this build.

  2. Performed full manual Office removal

    • Created dedicated uninstall directory.

    • Downloaded and deployed the Office Deployment Tool (ODT).

    • Built custom XML configuration for full removal of all Office components.

    • Executed silent configuration-based uninstall.

    • Removed residual Office folders and registry components preventing reinstallation.

  3. Prepared a full offline installer for Microsoft 365

    • Created folder structure and loaded ODT.

    • Built custom XML configuration for full Microsoft 365 Apps (64-bit, Current Channel).

    • Downloaded entire Microsoft 365 package (full offline suite).

    • Validated integrity of downloaded package.

  4. Installed Microsoft 365 from a different business tenant/account

    • Installed Microsoft 365 Apps using the fresh offline build.

    • Confirmed installation of all apps: Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint, OneNote, Access, Publisher, and shared components.

    • Activated product under the new business tenant credentials.

    • Verified apps launch correctly and are fully functional.

Title: 3CX Issue 800-841-3000   when we dial this number from the following extension 302 - 307 the call can not be made   but if we dial the same number from  506, 204, 207 the call goes through   please check and let me know
Notes:
As issue reported again by Mike, so called and follow up with SIP Vendor and worked and troubleshooted with them so they have identified that As suspected, the destination number is rejecting the call. Please see the reply from them:
‘It appears that 1-800-841-3000 does not accept calls from toll-free numbers. Calling 1-800-841-3000 from non-toll-free numbers works fine. We cannot force the owner of 1-800-841-3000 to accept calls they do not want to receive. Some companies block toll-free numbers from calling them as they pay a higher per-minute fee.’ Solution: Please use a local number as the caller ID when dialling this number, and the calls should connect successfully.  then he have few questions regarding the DID and inbound call issues and provided him all the information.

Title: unable to connect to sftp.viaautofinance.com
Notes:
As per handover from Ajay its was still not working so troubleshooting the issue but had no luck so took the backup of the VM from HQ and uploaded to the Onedrive and download at DC and restored the VM at DC with VAF network and folder and assigned the tag and configured the IP address and verified internet is working fine and then ping and nslookup the url sftp.viaautofinance.com and its still directed to HQ IP address so tried to login into the DNS provider portal and then changed the routing to HQ IP to DC and then tried to ping and its giving the correct the information The issue started when the SFTP connection to sftp.viaautofinance.com failed, with the error message stating, "The server rejected SFTP connection, but it listens for FTP connections." Initially, DNS resolution was verified, and the domain was resolving correctly to the public IP (66.81.19.90). The Windows Firewall rule for port 22 was confirmed to be active, allowing inbound SFTP traffic. I also ensured that Cerberus FTP Server was configured correctly to listen for SFTP connections on port 22, and the SFTP listener was active in the server settings.
To further investigate, I ran the telnet command to verify the connection on port 22, which failed, confirming that the issue was not isolated to a specific client. Additionally, I tested connectivity via WinSCP, but the connection attempt failed with the same error. After reviewing the Cerberus FTP Server configuration, I found that the trial license had expired, which likely caused the server to stop accepting SFTP connections.

The NAT rule on the Meraki firewall and the internal firewall settings were correctly forwarding traffic from the public IP to the internal server IP on port 22, which ruled out any issues with the network configuration. However, it was clear that the expired Cerberus FTP Server trial license was the root cause, as it likely disabled SFTP functionality. The next step will be to apply a commercial license or extend the trial to restore full SFTP functionality.

Title: unable to connect to sftp.viaautofinance.com
Notes: The issue was initially suspected to be related to the expired Cerberus FTP Server trial license, so I opened a case with the support team to request a trial key extension. While awaiting their response, I continued troubleshooting and decided to create a new VM identical to the current setup, ensuring all configurations were replicated. I installed Cerberus FTP Server on the new VM, completed the necessary configurations, and created the required user accounts. After setting everything up, I tested the server and confirmed that SFTP connections were successfully established and everything was working fine. In parallel, I received the trial key from the support team, which is valid for 15 days, and applied it to the new VM, restoring full functionality.

Title: My DISK
Notes:
Upon receiving reports of degraded performance on the client's MyDisk environment, I responded promptly and initiated a thorough investigation. The reported symptoms included significant response latency, delays in file opening, and disruptions to file synchronization — all of which were directly impacting the client's day-to-day business operations.

I took immediate action by performing an initial standard reboot of the MyDisk system, which yielded minor improvements. Recognizing that the issue persisted, I escalated my approach and executed a full clean reboot of the environment to ensure a complete system refresh and eliminate any lingering processes contributing to the degradation.

Despite the clean reboot showing normal load metrics from my monitoring perspective, I acknowledged the client's continued experience of lag and proactively engaged the vendor to conduct a deeper root-cause analysis and committed to delivering a long-term, sustainable solution — not just a temporary fix.

Following continued monitoring, I identified resource utilization running below 20%, ruling out capacity overload as the primary cause and narrowing the scope of investigation.

Additionally, I swiftly addressed a separate Cisco Umbrella access concern raised by the client, which was resolved promptly without any impact to operations. The client want us to check with the issue tonight and make sure this issue wont persist long. Giving it to next team.

Title: SERVER Alert - Trouble - VAF-DC-TSVDI-GI: (10.100.103.65) - Disk Utilization is 95%, threshold value for this monitor is 60%
Notes: As per the alert connected to  VAF-DC-TSVDI-GI, download and install TreeSize, a disk space management tool that provides a detailed overview of the disk usage across folders and files on the system. After installation, TreeSize was run to analyse the disk. TreeSize scans the system and generates a report showing the size of all folders and files in a hierarchical structure so carefully review all folders and files occupying excessive space. Based on the review, we proceeded to clear unnecessary files that were identified as no longer needed. This included: Temporary Files: Files in the system's temporary folder are often created by applications during installation, updates, or other processes; clear these temporary files to free up space. Recycle Bin: emptied the Recycle Bin, where deleted files are temporarily stored. This ensured that deleted files were permanently removed from the disk and no longer took up space. Downloaded Items: System error memory dump files, Old Windows updates, previous Windows installations other system cache files. 35% of the space is free. Now Sufficient Free Space Available.
